title: Palabruno: From Founder Idea to Launch
description: Helped a nontechnical founder turn a rough language-learning product idea into a launched Spanish reading MVP with mobile subscriptions, teacher payments on the web, app store launch assets, UX polish, and clearer positioning.
seoTitle: Palabruno Case Study | Spanish Reading MVP Launch
seoDescription: How Palabruno moved from nontechnical founder idea to a launched Spanish reading MVP across iOS, Android, and web with payments and teacher workflows.
bestFit: Nontechnical founders who need help shaping the first commercial version of a product, not just hiring someone to implement a fixed spec.
deliveryHighlights: Mobile Spanish reader for iOS and Android
Teacher web dashboard for classes, students, and shared readings
Paid upgrade paths through RevenueCat mobile subscriptions and web payments for teachers
Public site, pricing page, legal pages, store assets, and handoff docs
outcomes: Launched iOS, Android, and web surfaces
Connected mobile subscriptions through RevenueCat and teacher payments through Stripe
Gave the founder a clearer teacher-led buyer path
constraints: The founder had a strong product idea and early prototype, but needed help deciding which parts belonged in a first commercial MVP.
The product had to serve two connected audiences: individual Spanish learners on mobile and teachers managing students from the web.
The MVP needed real payments and app-store readiness without pretending the product had to support enterprise scale on day one.
Late-stage feedback kept surfacing across UX, pricing, legal copy, performance, screenshots, and store-review requirements.
problem: Palabruno began as a strong founder idea: make Spanish reading feel lower-friction by letting learners tap a word, get translation, grammar, and pronunciation help, and stay inside the reading flow.

The harder question was not whether the app could be built, but rather what the first commercial version should be. A student reader alone was useful, but teachers were the clearer first buyer: a tutor could turn lesson material into level-appropriate readings, send them to students, and bring those students into the app.

Because the founder was nontechnical, the work needed more than implementation. The project needed product judgment, scope cuts, UX decisions, payment architecture, launch support, and enough marketing clarity to explain the product to teachers and students.
solution: I narrowed the idea into a launchable MVP with two connected surfaces: a mobile reader for students and a web workspace for teachers.

On mobile, the product centered on the reading loop: import or generate Spanish texts, tap words for support, save useful readings, share content, export PDFs, and upgrade to Premium through the app stores.

On web, the Teacher plan gave the product its first paid buyer path: create custom readings from lesson notes, manage students and groups, share texts, track recent shares, and keep teacher workflows separate from the mobile-first learner experience.

I shaped the Free, Premium, and Teacher tiers so the founder could validate real demand without forcing every user through the same paywall. Premium stayed on mobile subscriptions through RevenueCat; Teacher used Stripe on the web for tutors and small classes.

I also handled the launch surface around Supabase, RevenueCat, Stripe, App Store Connect, Google Play, legal pages, store assets, screenshots, release docs, public-site copy, and founder handoff so the product could be owned after launch.
result: Palabruno moved from founder idea to launch: the iOS app went live on the App Store, the Android app went live on Google Play, and the public launch pages were ready for real users.
The product can now take payments through the right channels: Premium through Apple and Google via RevenueCat, and Teacher through Stripe on the web.

The founder ended the engagement with a working cross-platform product, teacher dashboard, shared backend, launch documentation, store assets, and a clearer first market to test with real Spanish teachers.
This is the kind of work I want to do more of: help a nontechnical founder refine the idea, cut the MVP to the right shape, build the product, improve the UX and copy, and get it into the world.
proofIntro: Product screenshots from the launched MVP, showing the teacher workflow and launch-ready polish.

proofScreens.label: Teacher workspace
proofScreens.title: Teachers get a dedicated workspace for lessons and students.
proofScreens.caption: The web dashboard gives tutors one place to create readings, manage students, and prepare material for class.

proofScreens.label: Text creation
proofScreens.title: Lesson notes can become level-matched readings.
proofScreens.caption: Teachers can choose a topic, level, and length, then turn class material into Spanish reading practice.

proofScreens.label: Teacher roster
proofScreens.title: Student records and invites live in the product.
proofScreens.caption: The roster keeps student names, invite status, and next actions close to the reading workflow.

proofScreens.label: Student groups
proofScreens.title: Groups support recurring classes and tutoring cohorts.
proofScreens.caption: Teachers can organize students by lesson group and share readings with the right class faster.

proofScreens.label: Share history
proofScreens.title: Teachers can see what was sent and when.
proofScreens.caption: Recent shares make it easier to track assigned readings across email, links, and WhatsApp.
